title: Industrial Supply Chain Management Platform
entry_mechanism: Saudi software company building industrial SCM platform anchored by ARAMCO IKTVA supply chain digitalisation
executive_summary:
Industrial supply chain management — the software platforms that manage procurement, vendor performance, inventory, demand planning, and delivery scheduling for industrial companies — is a large professional services and software market that flows almost entirely to SAP, Oracle, and Infor in Saudi Arabia. Saudi Arabia's industrial programmes (PSAP, IKTVA, NIDLP) mandate supplier development and local content tracking — creating compliance data management requirements that are not well served by generic ERP platforms.

Node LG-4 targets a Saudi-developed industrial SCM platform specifically designed for Saudi IKTVA/NIDLP compliance: supplier local content tracking, Saudi industrial council certification management, Aramco IKTVA scoring integration, and Arabic-language supplier communication. The compliance requirement is the unique Saudi use case that foreign platforms serve poorly.

The platform has immediate demand from Saudi Arabia's 2,000+ IKTVA-registered suppliers and 500+ NIDLP-participating companies. Annual platform licensing revenue of $20–40 million is achievable within 3 years.

companies.name: SAP Ariba
companies.rationale: SAP Ariba is the dominant industrial procurement platform with Saudi Aramco deployment. Saudi SCM built on SAP APIs and extending Ariba with Saudi compliance modules.
companies.contact: SAP VP Supply Chain, Saudi Arabia

companies.name: Oracle SCM Cloud
companies.rationale: Oracle SCM is widely used in Saudi Arabia. Saudi compliance module development on Oracle platform.
companies.contact: Oracle VP Supply Chain, Middle East

companies.name: Infor CloudSuite Industrial
companies.rationale: Infor has industrial ERP with active Gulf manufacturing customer base.

companies.name: Jaggaer
companies.rationale: Jaggaer provides procurement platforms with government and industrial customer focus.

companies.name: Coupa Software
companies.rationale: Coupa provides spend management and supplier compliance tracking.

partners.name: Saudi Aramco / IKTVA Programme
partners.current: IKTVA compliance tracking using manual and spreadsheet tools; SAP fragmentation
partners.upgrade: Aramco as development partner for Saudi IKTVA SCM platform; IKTVA data API access; Aramco supplier network as pilot deployment base; Aramco 5-year platform licensing commitment

partners.name: GAMI (General Authority for Military Industries)
partners.current: PSAP local content management; some digital tools
partners.upgrade: GAMI PSAP compliance integrated into Saudi SCM platform; defence supplier local content tracking; GAMI mandate for PSAP suppliers to use Saudi SCM platform

partners.name: Elm Company
partners.current: Government digital platforms; business identity infrastructure
partners.upgrade: Saudi SCM platform built on Elm's government data infrastructure; Absher business identity integration; national supplier registry API

partners.name: KFUPM Industrial Engineering
partners.current: Supply chain engineering; operations research; industrial systems
partners.upgrade: Saudi supply chain platform design research; IKTVA compliance algorithm optimisation; Saudi SCM engineering programme
